title: Positive Breakout: These 8 stocks cross above their 200 DMAs
summary: As long as the stock is priced above the 200-day SMA on the daily time frame, it is generally considered to be an overall uptrend.

title: Sebi directs depositories to freeze promoter holdings during buyback period, new rules explained
summary: Sebi has directed depositories to freeze promoter holdings during company buyback periods.  This restriction will be in effect from board approval until the offer closes.  Promoters can still participate in buybacks through the tender offer route.  Existing pledges created before the buyback period can also be invoked.  Depositories must implement these operational systems by August 1.

title: Stocks in news: IndusInd Bank, Adani Green, Eternal, Maruti Suzuki, Bandhan Bank
summary: Indian markets closed marginally higher after balancing mixed global cues and positive earnings. Adani Energy Solutions reported significant profit growth, while Adani Total Gas saw a decline. Maruti Suzuki announced a price increase, and several companies will report quarterly results soon. Bandhan Bank and Indian Hotels also posted strong first-quarter financial performances. Investors are watching key heavyweight sectors and upcoming company announcements closely.

title: D-St set for a negative opening as GIFT Nifty signals weak start
summary: Domestic equities extended losses for a second consecutive session on Tuesday.  Higher crude oil prices and geopolitical tensions pressured the market significantly.  Analysts expect equities to remain range-bound amid ongoing global uncertainties.  The ongoing Q1 earnings season will drive stock-specific actions and investor sentiment.  The Indian rupee firmed slightly as diplomacy hopes eased oil price concerns.

title: These 9 banking stocks have an upside potential of up to 27% in 1 year, according to analysts
summary: We live in a world where one statement or one missile can cause crude oil prices to spike. In such times, expecting rational behaviour in the equity (or commodity) market is probably expecting too much. At this juncture, investors need to develop the ability to either keep out of the market or invest only with a long-term perspective. title: SBI Funds' ESOPs mint fortunes for top executives
summary: SBI Funds Management executives received significant value from employee stock options after the company's listing. Joint CEO Devinder Pal Singh holds shares and outstanding options worth over ₹160 crore. Chief Investment Officer Srinivasan Rama Iyer's stake and ESOPs together exceed ₹165 crore. Other senior officials also saw substantial gains from their allotted employee stock options.

title: RBI plans unified code for foreign investment in equity instruments
summary: The Reserve Bank of India proposed new rules for overseas fund flows. These draft norms will replace existing regulations governing foreign investment. The framework clarifies eligible entities and defines foreign-controlled entities clearly. It also retains the distinction between direct and portfolio foreign investments. The proposed rules aim to streamline foreign investment into Indian markets.

title: FPIs stay in buying mode, consumer services lead sectoral inflows in first half of July
summary: Overseas investors became net buyers in the first half of July, marking a strong inflow period. Consumer services, metals, and healthcare sectors attracted the most foreign investment. IT sector saw marginal buying after months of consistent selling by foreign investors. Banks also received significant inflows, continuing their positive trend from June.

title: SBI Funds makes strong D-St debut, ends 6.3% above IPO price
summary: SBI Funds Management debuted on the stock market at a premium price.  The company raised over ₹9,813 crore through its initial public offering.  SBI plans no further stake dilution in the asset management company.  The fund house is investing in new Alternative Investment Funds capabilities.  SBI Funds aims to expand its reach into B30 markets.

title: KKR-backed Serentica Renewables lands massive $1.19B despite project delays and debt refinancing
summary: Serentica Renewables has secured over $1.19 billion across three financing transactions. This funding will accelerate capacity expansion and support delayed renewable energy projects. A $345 million loan comes from the Asian Development Bank and other lenders. MUFG underwrote a $397 million green term loan now being syndicated. The company aims to supply over 50 billion units of clean energy annually.

title: FCNR(B): Data show banks have raised $20.7 bn under scheme; $10 bn more may flow in
summary: The optimism follows RBI's disclosure that banks have collectively garnered $20.7 billion under its special incentive window, a pace that has already eased earlier concerns the scheme was struggling for traction. Of the total, FCNR(B) deposits accounted for the largest share at $17.4 billion, mobilised within six weeks of the scheme becoming operational. This was followed by overseas foreign currency borrowings (OFCBs) at $2 billion and external commercial borrowings (ECBs) at $1.3 billion.

title: Jana SFB reports ₹169-crore gap in FY25 gross NPAs
summary: Jana Small Finance Bank disclosed a ₹169 crore divergence in gross non-performing assets. The bank's reported GNPAs were lower than the Reserve Bank of India's assessment. This divergence led to an additional ₹42 crore provisioning for the bank. Consequently, the bank's net profit for the fiscal year was reduced. The RBI audit identified under-reported NPAs, necessitating corrections by banks.

title: Banks lure NRIs with high-return FCNR(B) deposits, warn of leverage risks
summary: Banks are attracting NRI dollar deposits through RBI's FCNR(B) window. These deposits offer projected returns of thirteen to sixteen percent. Customers can borrow multiple times their capital to amplify these dollar yields. However, early redemption carries substantial penalties and lender control over pledged funds. Investors face liquidity constraints and regulatory risks with these leveraged products.

title: Katayama Warns of Bold Action as Yen Slides Past 163 Per Dollar
summary: Japanese financial authorities are ready to take action in the currency market if necessary, Minister of Finance Satsuki Katayama said, in comments that did little to support the beleaguered yen.

title: Oil Extends Gain as Trump Cools Prospects for Talks With Iran
summary: Oil extended gains after President Donald Trump played down the prospect of near-term talks with Iran while threatening broader strikes, as risks to global supply spread beyond the Middle East to the Black Sea.

title: High Yielders Pace Emerging-Market Currency Gains, Stocks Jump
summary: Emerging-market currencies were mixed on Tuesday, with high-yielding names outperforming as investors looked past the latest developments in the Middle East and focused on carry trades instead.

summary: Asian stocks today: South Korea led regional gains, with the Kospi surging around 6%, while the Kosdaq advanced 2%.

title: Korean Stocks Jump as Brokers Say Margin Unwind Nearing End
summary: South Korean stocks jumped, as the unwinding of leveraged bets that helped drive the benchmark nearly 30% below its peak appeared to be nearing its end.

summary: Gift Nifty was trading around 24,108 level, a discount of nearly 72 points from the Nifty futures’ previous close, indicating a negative start for the Indian stock market indices.

summary: US-Iran war: Brent crude futures gained 0.55%, or 50 cents, to trade at $91.51 a barrel, on Wednesday.

title: Months after  ₹6k crore IPO, Raajmarg Infra mulls another share sale
summary: The trust, which is backed by the government, is considering a qualified institutional placement or a follow-on public offer, the people said, asking not to be named because the discussions are private.

title: Indian conglomerates' new pitch to foreign investors: Build with us from day one
summary: Instead of waiting to sell stakes after projects are de-risked, India's largest business groups are increasingly bringing in foreign equity partners at the outset to share risk, reduce capital outlay and fund simultaneous expansion across sectors.

title: NaBFID zero-coupon bonds explained: Investment size, returns, maturity, tax rules to know
summary: NaBFID’s  ₹20,000 crore zero-coupon bonds offer investors a 10-year fixed-income option with no regular interest payouts. Here are the issue price, maturity value, expected returns, taxation rules and key factors to consider before investing in these infrastructure-focused bonds.

title: 8th Pay Commission talks gain momentum as fitment factor hinges on fiscal realities
summary: 8th Pay Commission: Fitment factor remains the biggest concern for central government employees and pensioners. Final recommendations are expected by mid-2027, and fiscal sustainability will influence revisions to salaries and pensions.
